clusters.title: CSE ends in green amid sharp foreign selling

versions.title: CSE ends in green amid sharp foreign selling
versions.deck: The Colombo stock market ended in green yesterday for the second day after a volatile trading session which saw sharp early gains followed by a steady decline amid heavy foreign investor selling.

clusters.title: Mastercard strengthens digital payments ecosystem with two major launches

versions.title: Mastercard strengthens digital payments ecosystem with two major launches
versions.deck: Mastercard has announced the launch of two new payment solutions in Sri Lanka—Soundbox and Cloud Commerce—marking a major step forward in expanding access to simple, secure, and affordable digital payments for small enterprises.

clusters.title: People’s Bank donates Rs. 300 m to Rebuilding Sri Lanka Fund

versions.title: People’s Bank donates Rs. 300 m to Rebuilding Sri Lanka Fund
versions.deck: People’s Bank has come forward to donate Rs. 300 million to the ‘Government’s Rebuilding Sri Lanka Fund’ to support the development of a multi-storey housing project in the Nuwara Eliya District, which is being constructed to resettle families affected by Cyclone Ditwah.

clusters.title: Nimal takes over leadership of PRASL with a top team

versions.title: Nimal takes over leadership of PRASL with a top team
versions.deck: Nimal Gunewardena, a veteran in PR and IMC was elected to the role of President of the PR industry body, the Public Relations Association of Sri Lanka, along with a high calibre Board of agency and corporate comms leaders at its second AGM held on 21 April.

clusters.title: Metal Factor names Yong Lye Peng Scholarship Winner

versions.title: Metal Factor names Yong Lye Peng Scholarship Winner
versions.deck: Metal Factor has named Sampath Karunaratne, Creative Director at MullenLowe Sri Lanka, as the recipient of the 2026 Yong Lye Peng Scholarship.

clusters.title: British Cosmetics in strategic partnership with Ethera Business for global expansion

versions.title: British Cosmetics in strategic partnership with Ethera Business for global expansion
versions.deck: British Cosmetics Ltd., a leading manufacturers of premium skincare, cosmetics, and personal care products, has entered into a strategic partnership with Ethera Business LLC to support a disciplined expansion into international markets.

clusters.title: Antyra Solutions secures Google Premier Partner status for third consecutive year

versions.title: Antyra Solutions secures Google Premier Partner status for third consecutive year

versions.deck: Two common characteristics of the twenty-first century smoker are: (1) they are well aware of the health-risks associated with their habit of choice; and (2) they have repeatedly tried to quit but somehow found themselves back at the mercy of a smoke.

clusters.title: Why systems thinking is the next step for clinical governance in Sri Lanka

versions.title: Why systems thinking is the next step for clinical governance in Sri Lanka
versions.deck: A child presents to a busy private clinic with persistent headaches. The consultation is brief. There is no structured documentation template, no escalation pathway and no system to ensure follow-up. Weeks later, the family seeks help elsewhere with worsening symptoms.

clusters.title: Jaswar outlines his plans

versions.title: Jaswar outlines his plans
versions.deck: Former Royalist Jaswar Umar has outlined a clear and progressive vision for the future of the National Olympic Committee, focusing on transparency, athlete welfare, and stronger collaboration with National Federations. He is one of the top candidates to take over the Presidency.

clusters.title: Symbolic governance and the cost of silence

versions.title: Symbolic governance and the cost of silence

It loses it in instalments - through unchallenged assumptions, unasked or unanswered questions, missed warning signals and the quiet comfort of believing that someone else is watching the controls. clusters.title: The financial fraud at NDB Bank

versions.title: The financial fraud at NDB Bank
versions.deck: The financial fraud of a staggering amount of Rs. 13.2 billion at NDB Bank is without precedent in Sri Lanka. It has sent shock waves throughout the industry and among investors. The possibility of recovery of the stolen funds seems to be very uncertain at this point of time. The NDB Board collectively have to take the ultimate responsibility for this serious financial fraud. clusters.title: New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ team to tour Sri Lanka

versions.title: New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ team to tour Sri Lanka
versions.deck: The Rangiri Dambulla Cricket Stadium will be the venue for New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ team’s six-match tour of Sri Lanka next month. The New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ side is due to arrive in Sri Lanka on 7 May and commence their tour with a 3-match WODI series on 12 May against Sri Lanka Women’s ‘A’ side. clusters.title: Sri Lanka to see slow growth, widening fiscal deficit: Fitch

versions.title: Sri Lanka to see slow growth, widening fiscal deficit: Fitch
versions.deck: Fitch Ratings has said that Sri Lanka’s recovery remains fragile despite improved macroeconomic fundamentals, with the Middle East energy shock now testing the gains achieved through stabilisation and reforms. In its latest Sri Lanka update on credit development, Fitch said growth is expected to slow, inflation to rise and the fiscal deficit to widen in 2026, even as the economy builds on stronger fundamentals following the 2022 crisis. clusters.title: World Bank sees Sri Lanka growth slowing to 3.6% in 2026 amid energy shock

versions.title: World Bank sees Sri Lanka growth slowing to 3.6% in 2026 amid energy shock
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s economic growth is expected to slow to 3.6% in 2026 as higher energy prices weigh on activity, although the economy will regain its pre-crisis size last seen in 2018, the World Bank said in its latest South Asia Economic Update April 2026.
